long_stay: Vocational/professional training leading to a qualification
Other studies with justified reason (e.g., further training, specialized professional studies)
qualifying_courses.notes: A residence permit cannot be granted if your studies mainly consist of online distance learning that does not require your physical presence in Finland.

types.name: Schengen (Short-Stay) Visa
types.notes: For courses under 90 days, such as short courses, seminars, language courses, or single university modules.

booking_methods.description: Online application submission for residence permits.
Appointment booking for identity verification and biometrics at Finnish missions abroad or service points in Finland.

steps.title: Receive Acceptance Letter
steps.description: Obtain an official letter of acceptance from a Finnish educational institution.

steps.title: Submit Online Application
steps.description: Create an account and apply via the Enter Finland portal.

steps.title: Attend Identity Verification & Biometrics
steps.description: Visit a Finnish embassy, consulate, or service point to verify identity and provide fingerprints.

steps.title: Await Decision
steps.description: Track the application status via Enter Finland.

steps.title: Receive Residence Permit Card
steps.description: If approved, the residence permit card is mailed to the embassy or your home, or collected in Finland.

required_documents.name: Certificate of Acceptance
required_documents.details: Official letter of acceptance from a Finnish educational institution.

required_documents.name: Health Insurance
required_documents.details: Policy covering medical and pharmaceutical expenses, with a deductible of no more than €300, valid for the entire duration of stay.

required_documents.name: Proof of Tuition Fee Payment or Scholarship
required_documents.details: Proof that you have paid the first-year fee or a scholarship letter.

required_documents.name: Passport Photo
required_documents.details: One recent passport-sized photograph (no more than 6 months old) or digital photo retrieval code.

required_documents.name: Academic Transcripts
required_documents.details: Transcripts from all previous educational institutions.

required_documents.name: Previous Educational Certificates
required_documents.details: Required if you are a minor or a vocational student.

financial_requirements.reference_basis: To cover accommodation, food, and other needs

residence_permit.notes: Finland issues the residence permit before you arrive. Post-arrival, you must visit the Digital and Population Data Services Agency (DVV) to register your place of residence and receive a Finnish personal identity code. Higher education degree students typically receive a continuous (A) permit, while other qualification students may receive a temporary (B) permit.

restrictions: Work for an annual average of 30 hours per week (equivalent to approximately 1,560 hours per year).
The 30-hour limit does not apply to diploma work or internships that are formally part of the degree program.

required_documents: Proof of academic progress (at least 45 ECTS credits per academic year)
Statement explaining reasons if fewer than 45 ECTS credits completed
permit_renewal.notes: Extended permits can only be applied for while you are inside Finland. You may be refused renewal if you do not make sufficient academic progress.

requirements: Graduation from a Finnish educational institution
Find a job, pursue a trade, or engage in business activities in Finland
Labor market testing is waived for those with a Finnish degree (for work-based permit)
post_study_work.notes: The job-seeking permit can be used in parts, with the last part ending within three years of when the first part started. Permanent residence is possible after four years on a continuous residence permit, including your study period, especially if you are employed post-graduation.

official_sources.name: Finnish Immigration Service (Migri)
official_sources.description: Official information on studying in Finland: migri.fi/en/studying-in-finland

official_sources.name: Finnish Ministry for Foreign Affairs
official_sources.description: Information on visas and Finnish missions abroad.

official_sources.name: Digital and Population Data Services Agency (DVV)
official_sources.description: For registering place of residence and obtaining a personal identity code.

official_sources.name: Migri Appointment Booking
official_sources.description: Booking system for appointments at Finnish missions and service points.

application_forms.name: Residence Permit Application for Studies (Form OLE_OPI)

tips.title: Apply Early
tips.body: Apply for your residence permit as soon as you receive your acceptance letter to ensure you receive it before your studies begin. July is an extremely busy period.

tips.title: Ensure Sufficient Funds
tips.body: Make sure the full required amount (€9,600 for one year) is in your own bank account when you submit your application. A letter of support from a relative is usually not enough; the cash must be accessible to you.

tips.title: Check Passport Validity
tips.body: Ensure your passport is valid for the entire period you are applying for.

tips.title: Maintain Academic Progress
tips.body: You must complete at least 45 ECTS credits per academic year to qualify for permit renewal. Track your progress throughout your studies.

tips.title: Adequate Health Insurance
tips.body: Ensure your insurance has a deductible of no more than €300 and covers medical and pharmaceutical expenses for the entire duration of your stay.

tips.title: Apply from Outside Finland
tips.body: As of 1 September 2024, a first residence permit application must be submitted from outside Finland. Applying from inside Finland will generally result in a negative decision.

tips.title: Correct Visa Type for Course
tips.body: If your course is entirely online and requires no physical presence in Finland, a study residence permit will be refused.

tips.title: Translate and Legalise Documents
tips.body: All attachments must be translated and legalised where necessary before submission; failing to do so causes delays or rejections.

tips.title: Avoid Unnecessary Consultancy
tips.body: You do not need to use fee-based consultancy services, as the Enter Finland portal guides you through the entire application process.

common_mistakes.consequence: May not receive your permit before the start of your studies, especially during peak seasons.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Start your residence permit application as soon as you receive your acceptance letter.

common_mistakes.mistake: Insufficient funds at the time of application
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Ensure the full required amount (€9,600 for one year) is in your bank account when you submit the application.

common_mistakes.mistake: Submitting a first permit application from inside Finland
common_mistakes.consequence: Generally results in a negative decision (as of 1 September 2024).
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Always submit the first residence permit application from outside Finland.

common_mistakes.mistake: Applying for the wrong visa type (e.g., for online-only courses)
common_mistakes.consequence: Your study residence permit will be refused.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Ensure your course requires physical presence in Finland and qualifies for a study residence permit.

common_mistakes.consequence: Causes delays or rejections of your application.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Ensure all attachments are translated and legalised where necessary before submission.

common_mistakes.consequence: Your permit renewal may be refused.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Complete at least 45 ECTS credits per academic year and track your progress throughout your studies.

common_mistakes.consequence: Your application may be rejected or you may face issues with medical expenses.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Ensure your insurance has a deductible of no more than €300 and covers the entire duration of your stay.

common_mistakes.mistake: Passport not valid for the entire study period
common_mistakes.consequence: Issues with permit validity or renewal.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Ensure your passport is valid for the entire period you are applying for.
